description: LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Vertical stack layout. Headline text at top center, product images in center mid-section with annotation callout labels pointing to the product, and a full-width CTA button at the bottom. PRODUCT: Two dark black/chrome earplugs shown at a 3/4 angle, floating above a shallow white bowl/dish. The earplugs have a metallic silver ring detail and mesh grille. They are centered horizontally, occupying roughly 40% of the ad height. HEADLINE: 'CAN'T SLEEP THROUGH THE NOISE?' in bold, black, uppercase sans-serif font (approx 22-26px equivalent), centered at the top ~15% of the ad. SUBTEXT: Four callout labels with curved arrow lines pointing to the earplugs: 'Always a perfect fit' (top-left), 'Reusable & washable' (top-right), 'All-night comfort' (bottom-left), '28 dB of Noise Reduction' (bottom-right). Each label is in a small regular-weight sans-serif font (~11px), black text. CTA: 'TRY ECHOS' in bold black uppercase sans-serif text, centered inside a rounded-corner white button with a thin black border, positioned at the very bottom of the ad, spanning about 50% width. COLORS: Background is a soft light blue-to-white gradient (top blue #D6E4F0 fading to white). Product is black/dark gray with silver accents. Text is black. Bowl is white/cream. SPACING: Generous whitespace above and below the product. Headline has ~20px top padding. CTA has ~20px bottom padding. MOOD: Clean, minimal, modern, sleep-focused. Problem stated as a question with the product as the implicit answer.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1) designed to look like a screenshot of a Facebook/Instagram DM conversation. The entire ad is styled as a social media message interface. At the top is a brand header bar with the brand avatar and name. Below is a message thread with a customer testimonial as a long text message bubble on the left, and two product photos on the right side arranged in a small 2-image grid. Bottom has a message input bar. PRODUCT: Two product photos shown in the message thread on the right side: one shows product bottles on a pink/coral background, the other shows products on a light blue background. The products appear to be Native brand deodorant/body care items in white packaging. Each photo occupies roughly 25% of the ad width, arranged side by side. HEADLINE: Brand name 'NATIVE' appears at the top in bold dark sans-serif text with a circular purple 'N' avatar to the left. Below that, 'Saved 7 months ago' in small gray text. SUBTEXT: The main body is a customer testimonial message from 'Tonya' (shown with a coral 'TA' avatar circle). The message is a long-form personal story about body odor struggles and how the product changed her life. Text is in a standard message font, black, regular weight, ~12px. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The social proof format itself serves as the call to action. COLORS: Background is white (message app interface). Header bar has a blue gradient (#4267B2 to #5B7BD5 Facebook-style blue). Message text is black. Avatars use coral/salmon (#E88B6E). Interface elements are standard gray (#999). SPACING: Standard messaging app spacing - tight message bubbles, standard padding. MOOD: Authentic, testimonial-driven, social proof. Designed to feel like a real customer conversation screenshot, not a polished ad. UGC-style format builds trust.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Two-section vertical split. Top ~45% has a dark/black background with headline text and a small stats card. Bottom ~55% shows a phone mockup displaying a website. PRODUCT: A smartphone (iPhone-style with black bezel) showing the BudCraft website with three colorful canned beverage products. The phone is centered, slightly tilted, occupying about 60% width and 50% height of the ad. The phone screen shows a yellow banner with 'FREE SHIPPING OVER C$0' text and three cans in blue, yellow, and pink packaging. HEADLINE: 'Get a FREE UX and Site Speed Audit' in white text at the top on the dark background. 'FREE UX' is in bold/heavy weight, the rest is regular weight, all sans-serif, centered, ~20px equivalent. SUBTEXT: A small rounded card/pill element between the headline and phone shows 'Conversion rate 5.39%' on the left and 'View report +34%' in green on the right, simulating an analytics dashboard snippet. This card has a white background with subtle rounded corners. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The headline itself is the offer/CTA. COLORS: Top section background is very dark charcoal/black (#1A1A1A). Headline text is white. The stats card has white background with dark text and green accent (#22C55E) for the +34%. Phone screen shows yellow (#FFD700), blue (#0066CC), pink (#FF69B4) product packaging. SPACING: Tight spacing throughout. Headline has ~15px top margin. Phone overlaps the dark/light boundary. MOOD: Professional, tech-savvy, conversion-focused. Aimed at e-commerce brands wanting better website performance.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Left-weighted layout with product on the left side (~40% width) and benefit list on the right side (~55% width). Headline spans the top. Price/value proposition at the bottom left. Brand logo at bottom right. PRODUCT: A tall black-and-white Huel shaker bottle/container shown at a slight angle, occupying the left 40% of the ad from about 15% to 85% vertical height. The bottle is split to show layers of real food ingredients inside (grains, greens, eggs, fish, nuts) visible through a transparent cross-section effect, making the nutrition claims visually literal. HEADLINE: 'One meal of Huel Black Edition =' in dark/black sans-serif bold font at the top, spanning full width. 'Huel' and 'Black Edition' are in bolder weight. Font size ~18px equivalent. SUBTEXT: Four benefit lines stacked vertically on the right side, each with a small colored circular icon (green, yellow, orange, orange): 'More protein than 6 eggs', 'More potassium than a banana', 'More omega-3 than a salmon fillet', 'More vitamin C than an orange'. Each line has 'More [nutrient]' in bold colored text and 'than a [food]' in regular black text, ~13px. The word 'More' and the nutrient name use green/teal color. CTA: No button CTA. Bottom left shows '34 meals for $2.50 per meal' in bold green text as the value proposition. Brand logo 'Huel' in small black text at bottom right with a registered trademark. COLORS: Background is white/very light gray (#FAFAFA). Text is dark charcoal/black. Benefit keywords use teal/green (#00816D). Price text is the same green. Product is black and white with colorful food layers inside. SPACING: Clean whitespace margins on all sides (~20px). Benefit items have ~15px vertical spacing between them. MOOD: Clean, nutritional, informative. Problem (meal replacement complexity) solved by showing ingredient equivalents. Scientific yet approachable.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 2:1 or 16:9). Full-bleed lifestyle photography background showing a bedroom scene. Headline text overlaid at the top center, subtext below it, and a CTA button at the bottom center. Small brand logo at top left. PRODUCT: A white/cream quilted summer doona (comforter/quilt) draped over a bed in a styled bedroom setting. The product is shown in-context, occupying the lower 60% of the image as part of the bedroom scene. A woman is partially visible sitting on the bed. HEADLINE: 'YOUR WIFE IS HOT' in large, bold, white uppercase sans-serif font, centered in the upper portion over the photo. Very bold/heavy weight, ~28px equivalent. This is an attention-grabbing, humorous/provocative hook. SUBTEXT: 'GET HER AUSTRALIA'S COOLEST SUMMER DOONA' in smaller white uppercase sans-serif text directly below the headline, ~12px. CTA: 'SHOP THE SUMMER QUILT' in dark text on a light/white rounded-corner button, centered at the bottom of the ad, ~40% width. COLORS: Background is a warm bedroom photograph with beige/cream/wood tones. Text is white with a slight shadow for legibility over the photo. CTA button is white/cream with dark text. Brand logo 'bambi' appears in white at top left. SPACING: Headline is positioned about 25% from top. Subtext immediately below. CTA at about 85% from top. MOOD: Playful, cheeky, humorous. Uses a double-entendre headline (hot = temperature) to grab attention. Lifestyle/aspirational bedroom setting. Problem (overheating in sleep) solved with humor.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Dynamic diagonal layout with white and yellow/gold sections. The background is split: white occupies the top-left area and yellow (#FFC107 / golden yellow) fills the bottom-right in a curved/organic shape. Headline text at top right area, subtext below it, CTA button in the middle, and a large product image anchored at the bottom center. A small decorative heart-in-speech-bubble icon at the left edge, and a checkmark badge icon at the top right. PRODUCT: A pair of red canvas sneakers (Converse-style low-tops) with white rubber soles and white laces, shown at a dynamic angle as if tossed/floating. They occupy roughly the bottom 50% of the ad, centered, casting a subtle shadow on a white circular platform/surface. HEADLINE: 'New' in a pink/magenta cursive/script font at top-center, followed by 'PRODUCT' in very large, bold, black uppercase sans-serif (extra bold weight, ~40px equivalent). The word 'PRODUCT' dominates with heavy visual weight. SUBTEXT: 'GET THE LATEST AND MOST COMPLETE ITEMS ONLY AT OUR PLACE!' in small uppercase sans-serif text (~9px), centered below the headline, dark gray/black. CTA: 'SHOP NOW' in bold white uppercase text inside a rounded-pill hot pink/magenta button (#E91E63), centered horizontally, positioned between the subtext and the product image. Button is approximately 40% width. COLORS: White (#FFFFFF) and golden yellow (#FFC107) split background. Product is red (#E53935) with white soles. CTA button is hot pink/magenta (#E91E63). 'New' text is pink. 'PRODUCT' text is black. Small decorative elements in pink and blue. SPACING: Tight composition. Elements are close together. Product bleeds slightly past the bottom edge. MOOD: Energetic, vibrant, retail/e-commerce. Playful and youthful. Bold colors demand attention.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 16:9 or 2:1). Horizontal layout with a soft beige/cream textured background. 'SUPER SAVER COMBO' headline at the top center. Two product bottles arranged side by side in the center, with pricing information to the right. Brand logo at top right corner. PRODUCT: Two skincare/wellness bottles - a yellow/amber bottle of 'Turmeric Rosehip Mukhaleya' oil on the left, and a white spray bottle of 'Lavender Flora Jal' on the right. Both have brand labels. They are centered, each occupying about 20% width, positioned in the middle 60% of the ad height. HEADLINE: 'SUPER SAVER COMBO' in large serif/decorative gold-toned text at the top center. 'SUPER SAVER' is larger, 'COMBO' slightly smaller below it. SUBTEXT: Product names appear near each bottle in small serif text. 'TURMERIC ROSEHIP MUKHALEYA' on the left, 'LAVENDER FLORA JAL' on the right with brief benefit descriptions. CTA: No explicit CTA button. Pricing shown on the right: original price (crossed out, ~876 in strikethrough) and sale price '399' in bold, both in dark/red tones. COLORS: Background is warm cream/beige (#F5E6D3) with a subtle aged paper texture. Text is dark brown/gold. Price uses red/coral for the sale price. Products have yellow/amber and white/green packaging. SPACING: Moderate spacing. Products have breathing room between them. MOOD: Ayurvedic, natural, premium yet affordable. The combo deal format emphasizes value. Warm earth tones convey natural/organic positioning.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Centered circular composition. Headline text at top spanning full width. A large thin circular ring frames a central photograph of a pregnant woman. Four problem callouts are placed around the circle in small oval/pill badges at the four quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right). PRODUCT: No physical product shown. The ad is for pregnancy pillows (the brand sells premium ones). The pregnant woman in the center is shown from chest to belly, wearing a light blue chambray shirt, with one hand raised in a 'stop' gesture and the other on her belly. She occupies about 60% of the ad height within the circle. HEADLINE: '4 REASONS Why you should avoid Cheap Pregnancy Pillows' at the top. The '4' is an enormous bold 3D-style number in coral/terracotta color (#CC6644), about 60px equivalent, positioned at the top-left. 'REASONS' is in large bold dark brown uppercase sans-serif next to it. 'Why you should avoid' is in regular weight cursive/script font below. 'Cheap' is in regular weight and 'Pregnancy Pillows' is in bold, both dark brown sans-serif. SUBTEXT: Four oval pill-shaped badges with coral/pink (#D4776B) backgrounds and white text positioned around the circular frame: 'Lack of Support' (bottom-left), 'Short Lifespan' (top-right), 'Dangerous Recycled Filling' (bottom-left), 'Produces More Heat' (bottom-right). Each has a small relevant icon. Text is ~10px white, with key words in bold. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a very soft peach/blush (#FDE8DC). Circle ring is coral/terracotta (#CC6644). Problem badges are coral pink (#D4776B) with white text. The large '4' has a 3D shadow effect in darker coral. Woman wears light blue. SPACING: The circular frame provides natural spacing. Badges are evenly distributed around the circle. Generous padding on all edges. MOOD: Educational, warning-style. Positions cheap competitors as dangerous/inferior. The 'stop' hand gesture reinforces the warning message. Feminine, warm color palette for pregnancy audience.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 16:9). Two-column layout with a strong diagonal split. Left column (~55%) has a red background with white headline text. Right column (~45%) has a white/light background with a phone mockup and dark text CTA. PRODUCT: A smartphone (shown at a slight tilt) displaying the Bradesco banking app interface with a red-themed UI showing loan/payment options. The phone occupies the right ~40% of the ad, centered vertically in the right column. HEADLINE: 'EMPRESTIMO HOJE. 1a PARCELA DAQUI A 3 MESES.' (Portuguese for 'Loan today. First installment in 3 months.') in bold white sans-serif uppercase text on the red background, left-aligned. '3 MESES' is the largest text element. Font ~18-24px equivalent. SUBTEXT: On the right side, in dark/black bold sans-serif text: 'ABRA SUA CONTA PELO APP.' ('Open your account through the app.'). The Bradesco logo (red tree icon) appears at the top right. CTA: The subtext itself functions as the CTA directing to the app. No separate button. COLORS: Left section is Bradesco brand red (#CC0000). Right section is white/light gray. Text on red is white; text on white is dark charcoal/black. Bradesco logo uses red. Phone screen shows red-themed app UI. SPACING: Clean split layout. Text is left-aligned on the red side with comfortable margins. Phone has natural whitespace around it. MOOD: Corporate, trustworthy banking. Problem (needing money now) with solution (loan with delayed payments). Bold red conveys urgency and brand recognition.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Side-by-side comparison layout split vertically down the middle. Left half features the brand product (Magic Spoon), right half features a generic competitor ('Others'). Top section shows product photos with brand names/labels. Bottom section shows a 3-row nutritional comparison grid with alternating row backgrounds. PRODUCT: Left side: a white bowl of colorful ring-shaped cereal (like Froot Loops style) with a black spoon, shot from above, surrounded by a purple/violet circular highlight ring. Right side: a muted pink/mauve bowl of plain brown/chocolate cereal puffs, shown smaller and less appealing. HEADLINE: 'MAGIC SPOON' logo in stylized purple text with a registered trademark symbol at top-left. 'Others' in plain gray serif text at top-right. No traditional headline - the comparison format is the hook. SUBTEXT: Three comparison rows spanning full width, each with the Magic Spoon value on the left (in bold purple) and competitor value on the right (in regular gray/dark text): Row 1: '0g' vs '12g' for 'SUGAR' (center label). Row 2: '4-5g' vs '30g' for 'NET CARBS'. Row 3: '12-14g' vs '1g' for 'PROTEIN'. The center column labels (SUGAR, NET CARBS, PROTEIN) are in uppercase gray sans-serif. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The stark nutritional contrast serves as the persuasion mechanism. COLORS: Background is a soft gradient from light blue (#E0F0FF) on the left to light pink/lavender (#F0E0F0) on the right, creating a subtle brand vs. generic visual distinction. Magic Spoon values are in deep purple (#6B21A8). Competitor values are in dark gray. The cereal bowl highlight ring is purple. Comparison row backgrounds alternate between very light purple/white. SPACING: Clean grid layout. Rows are evenly spaced with ~15px padding. Product photos have equal vertical space. Generous margins on all sides. MOOD: Health-conscious, comparative, confident. The visual contrast (colorful vs. plain, purple vs. gray) makes the brand look clearly superior. Data-driven persuasion.

LAYOUT: Wide rectangular format (roughly 16:9 or 2:1). Full-bleed dark photograph of a woman's lower body (hips/thighs) wearing dark leggings, shot from behind. Text overlay on the right side. Brand logo watermark at bottom-left corner. PRODUCT: Dark charcoal/black Merino wool leggings shown being worn. The leggings cover the entire background as a lifestyle product shot. A hand is placed on the hip, showing the fit and fabric texture. The phone pocket detail is visible on the thigh. HEADLINE: 'SAY HELLO TO' in small uppercase light gray/white text at top-right, followed by 'Piper & Stella' in large white serif/mixed font below it. 'Piper & Stella' is the brand/product line name, ~24px equivalent. SUBTEXT: 'Our Duralife Merino Wool Leggings in Two Different Styles!' in smaller white sans-serif text below the brand name, ~11px. A small white brand logo appears at bottom left. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a dark moody photograph - deep charcoal/dark gray tones of the leggings and skin. Text is white/light gray for contrast against the dark background. SPACING: Text is right-aligned in the right ~40% of the ad. Comfortable padding from edges. MOOD: Premium, lifestyle, understated luxury. The dark photography and minimal text create an elevated fashion feel. Product introduction format.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Centered product showcase with headline at top and benefit badges arranged in a circular pattern around the products. Three product bottles lined up horizontally in the center. Six small icon badges are arranged around the products at roughly the 10, 2, 4, 8 o'clock positions and two at the sides. PRODUCT: Three Eva Naturals serum bottles arranged in a row at center: a dark amber bottle of 'Skin Restoring Retinol Serum' (left), a dark amber bottle of 'Vitamin C+ Serum' (center), and a dark amber bottle of 'Collagen Peptide Complex Serum' (right). Each has a different colored label accent (green, pink/coral, blue). They occupy roughly 60% width and 35% height of the ad. HEADLINE: 'EVA NATURALS' BEST SELLERS:' in bold dark sans-serif at top, with 'eva naturals' script logo above. Below: 'The Secret to Beautiful Radiant Skin!' in a slightly smaller bold serif/sans-serif mix. ~18px equivalent. SUBTEXT: Six circular icon badges with labels around the products: 'Cruelty Free' (bunny icon), 'Mercury Free' (Hg icon), 'Animal Oil Free' (leaf icon), 'DEA/MEA/TEA Free', 'Paraben Free' (chemistry icon), 'Polyethylene Glycol Free'. Each badge is a small white icon with dark text label. CTA: No explicit CTA button. COLORS: Background is a teal/dark cyan gradient (#1A7A7A to #2D9999) creating a rich, natural-health feel. Headline text is white. Product bottles are dark amber/brown. Badge icons are white/light on the teal background. Label accents on bottles are green, pink, and blue. SPACING: Products are evenly spaced in the center. Badges are symmetrically arranged around the products with ~20px gaps. Comfortable top/bottom margins. MOOD: Clean, natural, trustworthy. The certification badges emphasize safety and purity. Teal color conveys health and nature. Premium skincare positioning.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Clean vertical split down the middle - left half is light blue (#D6E8F5), right half is light pink (#F5D6E0). Left side lists problems, right side shows the solution product. Brand logo at bottom-right corner. PRODUCT: A large white cylindrical supplement container labeled 'Obvi Collagenic BURN ELITE' with pink/magenta branding. The container occupies the right half of the ad, centered, taking up about 70% of the right column's height. Lid is white/silver. HEADLINE: 'PROBLEMS' in large bold black uppercase sans-serif on the left side at top (~24px). 'SOLUTION' in large bold black uppercase sans-serif on the right side at top (~24px). This creates the classic problem-solution framework. SUBTEXT: Three problem items listed vertically on the left side, each with a small black icon: 'PCOS BELLY' (with a belly/body icon), 'HORMONE IMBALANCES' (with a female body icon), 'LOW ENERGY' (with a person-at-desk icon). Text is bold black uppercase sans-serif, ~14px. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The product on the solution side is the implied CTA. 'obvi' brand logo in small pink text at bottom-right. COLORS: Left half: soft blue (#D6E8F5). Right half: soft pink-to-cream gradient (#F5D6E0 to #FFF0E8). Problem text is black. Product label uses hot pink/magenta (#E91E8C) on white. Icons are black. SPACING: Even vertical distribution of problem items on the left with ~30px spacing. Product is vertically centered on the right. Clean center dividing line. Generous margins. MOOD: Health-focused, feminine, empathetic. Acknowledges real health struggles (PCOS, hormones) then presents a simple solution. The blue-to-pink transition feels calming and supportive.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Vertical split down the middle - left half has a light blue (#D6E8F5) background, right half has a golden yellow (#F5C518) background. Each side has a brand name at top, product image in the middle, and three benefit/feature statements at the bottom. A white circular 'VS' badge sits at the center dividing line between the two products. PRODUCT: Left side: a cylindrical cream/white cardstock container with elephant illustrations and the 'first day' branding, with two gummy vitamins (red/orange) sitting in front. Right side: a plain generic white/gray supplement bottle with gray cap, representing generic kids vitamins. Both products are centered in their columns, ~30% height each. HEADLINE: 'first day' in large lowercase dark serif font on the left side at top. 'Most Kids Vitamins' in bold dark sans-serif on the right side at top. The 'VS' in a white circle (bold, ~16px) sits at the center overlap of the two products. SUBTEXT: Three rounded-pill statement badges on each side below the products. Left (first day): 'Made with 80% less sugar', 'Made to fill in the gaps in a kid\'s diet', 'Backed by modern science' - white pills with dark text. Right (competitors): 'Sugar free or full of sugar', 'Made with 100% or more of daily nutritional value', 'Made with outdated formulas' - white pills with dark text. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The comparison format drives the persuasion. COLORS: Left column: soft light blue (#D6E8F5). Right column: golden yellow (#F5C518). Text is dark charcoal/black throughout. Statement pills are white with rounded corners. VS circle is white with dark text. The first day packaging is warm cream/white with colorful accents. SPACING: Symmetric layout - both columns mirror each other in structure. Product images are vertically centered. Statement pills have ~10px vertical spacing. Even margins throughout. MOOD: Parental, educational, trust-building. The VS comparison clearly positions the brand as the thoughtful, modern choice vs. generic alternatives. Warm, child-friendly colors.

LAYOUT: Small square format (1:1). Full-bleed product photography background showing multiple products arranged on a red/coral surface with green leaf decorations. A large centered semi-transparent white circle overlays the center with text inside. A small CTA button sits at the bottom of the circle. PRODUCT: Multiple bath/body products (bottles, jars) from what appears to be an organic/natural brand are scattered around the frame on a red surface with fresh green leaves/herbs. Products have green, amber, brown, and white packaging. They frame the central text circle. HEADLINE: Inside the white circle overlay: 'Time for a refill? Subscribe & save 15%!' in dark serif/sans-serif text, centered. 'Time for a refill?' is on the first line, 'Subscribe & save 15%!' on the second. ~14px equivalent. CTA: 'Save Now!' in a small dark green or black rounded pill button at the bottom of the white circle. White text on dark background, ~30% of circle width. COLORS: Background surface is red/coral (#CC3333). Products have various natural-tone packaging (green, amber, brown). Decorative leaves are bright green. Central overlay is white with ~85% opacity. Text is dark charcoal/black. CTA button is dark green/black. SPACING: Products frame the edges. Central circle occupies about 50% of the ad. Text is centered within the circle with ~15px padding. MOOD: Natural, organic, subscription-focused. The refill messaging implies sustainability and repeat purchase. Festive red background with green accents creates an inviting, seasonal feel.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1) designed to look like a magazine clipping or editorial feature on a light gray background. A white slightly-rotated card/clipping sits centered on the gray background with a drop shadow. The card contains: publication masthead at top, product photo in the middle, article headline below, and a pull quote at the bottom. PRODUCT: A single Glossier lipstick (Generation G or similar) in a pink shade, shown uncapped at an angle with its pink tube/case beside it. The lipstick sits on a light surface with soft shadows. It occupies the center ~60% of the card. HEADLINE: 'BYRDIE' in large bold black uppercase serif/editorial masthead font at the top of the card (~28px), centered. Below the product photo: 'These Are the Best Beauty Products Under $25' in bold black serif font, centered, ~16px. SUBTEXT: A pull quote below the headline in smaller italic serif text, ~10px, gray/dark: a brief editorial quote about the product. 'By Anna Lewski' attribution in small text below. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The editorial/press format serves as social proof and implied endorsement. COLORS: Outer background is medium gray (#B0B0B0). Card is white (#FFFFFF) with a subtle drop shadow. Text is black. Lipstick is pink/coral. The card has a very slight rotation (1-2 degrees) for a casual, clipped-out feel. SPACING: The card has ~20px internal padding. Product photo has generous whitespace around it. The overall ad has ~30px margins around the card. MOOD: Editorial, press-worthy, aspirational. The magazine clipping format leverages third-party credibility. Minimal and elegant, feels like earned media rather than paid advertising.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Two-section vertical layout. Top ~60% is a lifestyle photo of a man (shirtless, smiling, using the product). Bottom ~40% is a white card section with a social media-style testimonial (username, verified badge, review text). A small product image is inset in the top-right of the photo. PRODUCT: A Manscaped Lawn Mower 5.0 body groomer/trimmer shown in two ways: 1) being held/used by the man in the lifestyle photo (partially visible), and 2) as a small product shot inset in the upper-right corner showing the trimmer and its dark packaging/box (~15% of ad width). The product is black/dark with metallic accents. HEADLINE: No traditional headline. The social media handle '@darnell.davis' with a blue verified checkmark serves as the attribution header for the testimonial. SUBTEXT: The testimonial review text in the bottom white section: 'Great product. Light weight and performs beautifully. My trust was in the air from other products with cutting but the lawn mower 5.0 really left me wanting to use it again.' in regular black sans-serif text, ~12px. CTA: No explicit CTA button. The authentic testimonial format is the persuasion mechanism. COLORS: Top photo has warm skin tones and a bathroom/mirror setting with neutral beige/warm lighting. Bottom section is clean white (#FFFFFF) with black text. Verified badge is blue (#1DA1F2 Twitter-style). Product packaging is dark purple/black with gold accents. SPACING: Photo fills the top section edge-to-edge. Testimonial card has ~15px padding. Username is left-aligned. Text is left-aligned with comfortable line height. MOOD: Authentic, masculine, UGC-style. The real person + verified review format builds trust. Casual, personal tone addresses grooming insecurity with a confident, positive review.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Three horizontal bands/sections. Top band (~25%): black background with bold headline text. Middle band (~15%): yellow background with subheadline text. Bottom band (~60%): split into two halves showing a side-by-side food comparison. PRODUCT: A bowl of Immi Ramen shown on the left side of the bottom section - a dark bowl with ramen noodles, egg, and toppings shot from above. On the right side, a Cobb Salad in a white plate for comparison. Each food item occupies ~50% of the bottom section width. HEADLINE: 'BODY by RAMEN.' in the top black band. 'BODY' is in large bold white sans-serif (~30px). 'by' is in smaller yellow italic/script text. 'RAMEN.' is in large bold yellow sans-serif (~30px). The period adds emphasis. SUBTEXT: In the yellow middle band: 'BECAUSE OUR RAMEN TASTES BETTER WITH 1/2 THE CALORIES.' in bold black uppercase sans-serif, centered, ~12px. Below in the bottom comparison section: 'IMMI RAMEN' in bold black text over the ramen image with '300 CALORIES' in very large bold black text (~28px). 'Cobb Salad' in regular gray text over the salad with '600 Calories' in large gray text (~28px). CTA: No explicit CTA button. The calorie comparison is the persuasion. COLORS: Top band is black (#000000) with white and yellow (#FFD700) text. Middle band is yellow (#FFD700) with black text. Bottom-left has a dark/moody food photo background. Bottom-right has a light/white background. The calorie numbers create visual contrast (black bold vs. gray regular). SPACING: Tight horizontal bands with no gaps. Food photos fill their sections edge-to-edge. Text is centered within each band. MOOD: Bold, confrontational, playful. Challenges the assumption that ramen is unhealthy by comparing it to a 'healthy' salad. The contrast between the indulgent ramen visual and the calorie number creates surprise.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Gradient background with a centered headline question at the top, two side-by-side comparison cards in the middle, and a full-width CTA button at the bottom. PRODUCT: No single product shown. Two food photographs are presented in rounded-corner white cards for comparison: Left card shows a colorful healthy bowl (avocado, turkey, lettuce wrap with various vegetables) shot from above. Right card shows a take-out burger with fries. Each card occupies ~45% width with a small gap between them. HEADLINE: 'WHY YOU\'RE NOT LOSING WEIGHT?' in bold dark sans-serif text at the top center, ~18px. 'LOSING WEIGHT' is in a larger, bolder, colored (teal/green) weight. The question mark makes it a hook. SUBTEXT: Each comparison card has: a price indicator ($ vs $$), star rating (4.5 stars vs 3 stars), a label pill ('balanced' in green vs 'less balanced' in orange/coral), and a bullet-point list of nutritional characteristics. Left card: 'Avocado & turkey lettuce wrap' with benefits like 'Low in calories & carbohydrates', 'High in vitamins and minerals', etc. in small green bullet points. Right card: 'Take-out burger' with downsides like 'High in calories, fat & sodium', 'May contribute to weight gain', etc. in small coral/red bullet points. Text is ~9-10px. CTA: 'TAKE THE TEST' in bold white uppercase text inside a large rounded-pill gradient button (purple-to-magenta gradient, #8B5CF6 to #EC4899), centered at the bottom, spanning ~50% width. COLORS: Background is a soft gradient from light green/mint (#E0F5E8) at top-left to light purple/lavender (#F0E0F5) at bottom-right. Cards are white with subtle shadows. Left card accents are green/teal (#10B981). Right card accents are coral/orange (#F97316). CTA button is a purple-to-pink gradient. Headline uses dark charcoal and teal. SPACING: Cards have ~15px internal padding. ~10px gap between cards. Headline has ~20px top margin. CTA has ~20px bottom margin. Bullet points have ~8px spacing. MOOD: Health-conscious, educational, quiz-funnel style. The comparison card format makes the healthy choice feel obvious. The 'Take the Test' CTA drives engagement through personalization. Soft gradients feel modern and wellness-oriented.

LAYOUT: Square format ad (1:1). Clean white background. Centered vertical stack: headline at top (~20%), product arrangement in the center (~55%), CTA button below products (~10%), and disclaimer text at the very bottom (~5%). A small curved arrow illustration points from the headline toward the product. PRODUCT: An AG1 (Athletic Greens) welcome kit arrangement showing multiple items: a large tan/kraft cardboard box with 'AG1' branding (centered, largest element), a dark green canister of AG1 powder (right), a green pouch/bag of AG1 (right of box), AG1 travel packets (left-front), a glass bottle/shaker with green liquid (far left), and a small green measuring scoop (center-front). The entire product spread occupies about 60% width and 40% height, centered. HEADLINE: 'Get your free AG1 Welcome Kit*' in dark charcoal/black sans-serif text, centered at the top. 'Get your free' is in regular weight (~20px). 'AG1' has a small registered trademark. 'Welcome Kit' is in the same weight. The asterisk references terms at the bottom. SUBTEXT: '*Offer valid for new subscribers only' in small dark gray text at the very bottom center, ~9px. CTA: 'Claim Offer' in bold white text inside a large rounded-pill dark green button (#1B5E3B / forest green), centered below the product arrangement, spanning ~40% width. The button has generous padding making it prominent. COLORS: Background is clean white (#FFFFFF). Text is dark charcoal (#1A1A1A). CTA button is deep forest green (#1B5E3B) with white text. Product packaging uses various greens (forest green canister, sage green pouch, kraft cardboard). The curved arrow is dark teal/green. AG1 branding consistently uses green. SPACING: Generous whitespace on all sides (~25px margins). Headline has ~30px top padding. Products are carefully arranged with natural overlapping. CTA has ~15px gap from products. Disclaimer has ~10px bottom padding. MOOD: Premium, health-focused, generous. The 'free welcome kit' offer feels abundant. Clean white background with green accents conveys freshness and health. The product spread looks valuable and aspirational. Minimal design lets the offer speak for itself.
